message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Tripadvisor 1,057 reviews (4.5/5, #12 of 281 Palm Springs); Yelp 1,079 reviews (4.2); OpenTable cited at ~6,437 diners (4.8) per third-party summary [UNVERIFIED-direct]; Google ~4.4
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Opened December 2004; same owner-operators (Andrew Manion Copley, Juliana Manion Copley, Greg Butterfield) ~21 years — stable independent ownership
- Credentials: ["OpenTable 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America (2024)","OpenTable Diners' Choice (2026)","OpenTable Top 100 Patio Dining in America [UNVERIFIED-year]","Restaurateurs of the Year — Desert Resorts Convention and Visitors' Authority [UNVERIFIED-year]","Chef Andrew Copley listed in James Beard Foundation chef archive (not an award)","Featured/cited in New York Times (described as 'emerging chef' / 'hottest place') [UNVERIFIED-direct]","Zagat ratings cited on own site [UNVERIFIED-direct]","Co-owner Juliana Copley: co-founder of Palm Springs Restaurant Week; president of Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'date night Palm Springs', 'romantic restaurants Palm Springs', 'upscale Palm Springs dining', 'uptown design district restaurants', 'patio dining Palm Springs'. Surfaces moderately for: 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'best restaurants Palm Springs'. Does NOT consistently surface for: 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (eclipsed by Spencer's, Workshop Kitchen, Le Vallauris, Mister Parker's, 4Saints), 'contemporary American Palm Springs' (mentioned alongside Eight4Nine, Trio, Jake's rather than as anchor). Notably absent from 2025 Michelin Recommended list for Palm Springs.
- Site structured data: Weak. copleyspalmsprings.com has NO JSON-LD / schema.org markup (no Restaurant, LocalBusiness, OpeningHoursSpecification, or Menu schema). Address (621 N Palm Canyon Drive) and phone (760-327-9555) appear as plain text and tel: link. Hours present only as human-readable prose ('Open at 5:30pm 7 Nights a Week through May'). Menu pages exist but not machine-parseable. Agents must rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs) for structured data.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's the information about Copley's on Palm Canyon's Tripadvisor ranking and reviews:

## Tripadvisor Rating and Ranking

Copley's on Palm Canyon is rated 4.5 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #14 of 309 restaurants in Palm Springs. The restaurant has 1,055 unbiased reviews.

## About the Restaurant

Copley's on Palm Canyon is an award-winning restaurant serving upscale American cuisine infused with traces of tropical and European flavors, and is located in part of the former Cary Grant estate, where guests may experience a sense of nostalgia while dining outside on the patio with stunning mountain views or enjoying the inviting atmosphere indoors.

Executive Chef Andrew Copley has an extensive 20-year culinary background at five-star restaurants and hotels across the world and is known for innovative dishes.

Based on the search results, here are the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Restaurants

Rooster and the Pig was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, and it serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with signature dishes including shaking beef and spicy chile okra.

Workshop Kitchen & Bar holds both a James Beard Foundation design award and Michelin recommendation, making it the city's most decorated fine dining venue.

The MICHELIN Guide has reached Palm Springs, with 4 Saints, the rooftop dining room at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs, being one of them, with indoor and outdoor seating and wide-open views.

Other notable fine dining options include:

- Le Vallauris, a cornerstone of fine dining in Palm Springs, offering elegant French cuisine set in a historic home with a beautiful garden patio and classic French dishes expertly prepared with the finest ingredients.

- Bar Issi offers a coastal Italian menu, from wood-fired pizzas with seasonal farmers' market toppings to housemade pastas and a Snake River Farms wagyu ribeye.

- Maleza, the onsite restaurant at The Drift Hotel, is one of the best places to eat in Palm Springs, with Chef Ysaac Ramirez's bright, flavor-driven dishes drawing on Mexico City influences.

Both Rooster and the Pig and Workshop Kitchen & Bar require advance reservations during peak season, November through April.

Based on the search results, here's what the Michelin Guide recommends for Palm Springs restaurants in 2025:

## Overview

While the Michelin Guide hasn't awarded stars to any Palm Springs restaurants yet, they have bestowed the honorary title of "Michelin Recommended" to eight foodie hotspots.

## Featured Michelin-Recommended Restaurants

**4Saints** - Stylishly perched on the seventh-floor rooftop of the Rowan Hotel, 4Saints serves "modern American coastal cuisine" with small and large plates showcasing seafood, local produce, and prime meats, paired with craft cocktails and California wines.

**Cheeky's Palm Springs** - This breakfast and lunch hotspot has everything on the menu made from scratch using local, farm-fresh ingredients, with a rotating weekly menu featuring inventive takes on classics like carnitas hash or chilaquiles with house-made chorizo.

**Tac/Quila** - This downtown hotspot gets high marks for bold flavors and thoughtful presentation, with a menu inspired by Mexico's Jalisco region featuring elevated classics like street tacos with lobster and seared ahi.

**The Colony Club** - Located inside the glamorous Colony Palms Hotel & Bungalows, the restaurant was developing a "new menu concept" to debut Fall 2025.

The other four Michelin-recommended restaurants mentioned include The Barn Kitchen, Boozehounds, Workshop Kitchen + Bar, and Johannes, each offering distinct culinary experiences ranging from farm-to-table to modern American cuisine.

I found several great options for romantic dining in Palm Springs for a date night in 2026. Here are some highly-recommended restaurants:

## Top Romantic Restaurants

According to Yelp's April 2026 update, top romantic restaurants include Clandestino, The Front Porch, Spencer's Restaurant, FARM, Miro's Restaurant, Copley's, Eight4Nine, The Tropicale, Bar Cecil, and Ash & Vine.

## Notable Recommendations

**FARM** - FARM is a French-inspired eatery offering farm-to-table dishes with a cozy patio adorned with lush greenery and floral arrangements. It's a traditional Provençal-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu of traditional European dishes using locally sourced produce, and the prix fixe dinner menu is served at a pace so you may enjoy your evening.

**Copley's** - Copley's is now open 7 days a week through June 2026, serving American cuisine infused with tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate with upscale dining either outside on the patio with mountain views or indoors.

**Le Vallauris** - Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is tucked against the San Jacinto Mountains with thoughtful detail and an intimate setting that feels like a charming French home.

**Lavender Bistro** - Lavender combines finest meats and seafood with fresh organic ingredients, offering dining in a warm cozy atmosphere or on a stunning tree-lined patio, and provides superb live music nightly.

These restaurants offer the romantic ambiance and cuisine perfect for a memorable date night in Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Establishments

Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta Cliffhouse, The Bistro, Oceana Restaurant, Lavender Bistro, Nobu - Indian Wells, and T&T Innovation Kitchen are among the top fine dining options in the area (as of March 2026).

### Notable Fine Dining Restaurants:

**The Steakhouse** - This Rancho Mirage resort restaurant is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and has won the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007.

**Copley's On Palm Canyon** - Located in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate, Copley's offers a mix of American and European cuisine focusing on seasonal ingredients, with al fresco dining in the charming courtyard or cozy indoor setting.

**The Purple Room Supper Club** - Offering a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment in Palm Springs, this venue is known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine.

**Oceana Restaurant** - Located in Palm Desert, Oceana offers fine dining where fresh seafood meets exquisite flavors and stunning ambiance.

**Chez Pierre Bistro** - Located in Palm Desert, this restaurant offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week takes place May 29 - June 7, 2026, offering residents and visitors 10 days of exquisite dining experiences throughout the nine-city oasis.

Based on the search results, Juliana Copley was one of the founders of Palm Springs Restaurant Week. She is also the president of the Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association.

In addition to her involvement with Palm Springs Restaurant Week, Juliana's career as an entrepreneur began in 2001, when she co-founded a highly successful Destination Management Company in Hawaii, which she sold several years later when she moved to Palm Springs to open a restaurant with her husband Andrew Copley and good friend Greg Butterfield.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Copley's in Palm Springs:

Copley's on Palm Canyon opened on December 7, 2004. Andrew Copley serves as owner and executive chef and Juliana manages the business operations.

The building was once the guesthouse of the estate belonging to legendary actor Cary Grant. The restaurant quickly became one of the premier eateries in Palm Springs, garnering both local and national recognition.

The restaurant has recently been recognized for its romantic dining experience, as it was ranked one of the top 100 romantic restaurants by OpenTable in 2024.

content.text: I have enough evidence to assess all the claims. Let me finalize.

confirms: Tripadvisor ~1,055-1,057 reviews and 4.5/5 rating (search returned 1,055 reviews; minor delta from claimed 1,057 within normal flux)
Yelp 1,079 reviews (4.2 rating not directly confirmed but review count matches Yelp listing exactly)
OpenTable 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America (2024) — independently confirmed via Visit Palm Springs editorial
Co-owner Juliana Copley as Palm Springs Restaurant Week co-founder and president of Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association — confirmed across multiple sources
Restaurateurs of the Year — Desert Resorts CVA — confirmed as 2009 (previously [UNVERIFIED-year])
OpenTable Top 100 Patio/Outdoor Dining — confirmed via news-press page (year not specified, image appears ~2022)
Site has NO JSON-LD or schema.org microdata; address/phone as plain text and tel: link; hours as prose; menu as PDF — fully confirmed by direct fetch

Does NOT surface as top-tier in 'best restaurants Palm Springs' lists — multiple 2026 fine dining lists (Time Out, OpenTable, USA Today's Restaurant of the Year coverage) center on Rooster and the Pig, Workshop Kitchen, 4Saints, Le Vallauris, Bar Cecil, Maleza, Bar Issi — Copley's not in top tier
Notably absent from the 8 Michelin-recommended Palm Springs restaurants (4Saints, Cheeky's, Tac/Quila, Colony Club, Barn Kitchen, Boozehounds, Workshop, Johannes) — confirmed
Surfaces for romantic/date-night queries — confirmed (Yelp top-10 romantic, Wanderlog romantic, OpenTable Diners' Choice Romance category)
Surfaces in Coachella Valley fine dining lists at mid-tier (mentioned alongside The Steakhouse, Purple Room, Oceana, Chez Pierre — not as top pick)
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking claimed as #12 of 281 — actual current Tripadvisor data shows #14 of 309 Palm Springs restaurants. Both rank and denominator are off.

OpenTable diners count claimed at ~6,437 — web search surfaced a figure of 7,585 reviews on OpenTable (higher than claimed). The original [UNVERIFIED-direct] flag was warranted; the actual number is materially higher.
Restaurateurs of the Year year is NOT unverified — it is documented as 2009 (Palm Springs Desert Resort Communities CVA). The [UNVERIFIED-year] flag should be removed and year stated.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable rating of 4.8 — review count was confirmable but the precise rating value was not surfaced in independent search
Google rating ~4.4 — not independently verified in this pass
Yelp 4.2 rating — review count confirmed but star rating not independently verified
New York Times specific quote 'emerging chef' / 'hottest place' — only confirmed via Copley's own news-press page, not via NYT archive directly (remains [UNVERIFIED-direct])
Zagat ratings — only cited on own site; Zagat ceased operations in current form, archival verification not possible in this pass
OpenTable Diners' Choice 2026 — site listed in Diners' Choice category but specific 2026 badge year not directly visible (OpenTable page timed out)
James Beard Foundation chef archive listing for Andrew Copley — not verified in this pass
Same owner-operators for 21 years (Andrew, Juliana, Greg Butterfield) — opening date and ownership trio confirmed but continuous ownership without change not explicitly verified
input.notes: Core thesis of the assessment is sound: Copley's is a real, well-reviewed, long-running independent that surfaces strongly for romantic/date-night queries but is eclipsed in 'best of' and Michelin-recommended lists by Workshop Kitchen, 4Saints, Le Vallauris, and newer entrants like Rooster and the Pig and Bar Cecil. Structured-data gap is fully confirmed by direct site fetch — no JSON-LD, no microdata, hours as prose, menu as PDF. Visibility gap score of 6 and convertible tier are defensible. Key corrections: (1) Tripadvisor rank is #14 of 309, not #12 of 281; (2) OpenTable review count is higher than the cited 6,437 (surfaced as 7,585); (3) Restaurateurs of the Year was 2009. Several credentials remain dependent on the restaurant's own news-press page rather than primary sources — NYT quote, Zagat, James Beard listing should remain flagged unless primary verification is added.
